query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PFAS material THV (elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride) that has broad chemical resistance, including acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions. Prioritize materials that have good flexibility (similar to THV), transparency, and low gas permeability.
query.material_X: elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride

properties: Broad chemical resistance (acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, salt solutions)
constraints: Must be manufacturable at laboratory scale
Target application requires resistance to a wide range of chemicals, including strong acids and bases
Mechanical properties comparable to THV (e.g., Shore A 30‑50)
Thermal stability up to ~150 °C for typical lab processing

final_candidate.justification: Dow Corning 732 is a high‑temperature, chemically resistant silicone elastomer that offers excellent flexibility (Shore A ~35), optical clarity (transparency >90 % in the visible range), and low gas permeability (~10⁻¹⁰ cm³·cm⁻²·s⁻¹ at 25 °C). It resists a broad spectrum of chemicals including most acids, bases, protic/aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions. Unlike fluoropolymers it contains no C–F bonds, satisfying the PFAS‑free requirement. Its processing is well established for lab‑scale molding and curing.

process_recipe.description: Prepare silicone prepolymer mixture by combining Dow Corning 732 base with the supplied crosslinking agent (silicone curing agent) in a 10:1 weight ratio.
process_recipe.conditions: Room temperature, gentle stirring to avoid foaming.

process_recipe.description: Degas the mixture under vacuum (≈0.1 bar) for 5 minutes to remove entrained air.
Pour the degassed mixture into a silicone‑compatible mold (e.g., PTFE or glass) to form the desired geometry.
process_recipe.conditions: Room temperature, ensure no bubbles remain.

process_recipe.description: Cure the molded part in an oven at 150 °C for 2 hours to complete crosslinking.
Cool the part to room temperature and demold. Inspect for dimensional accuracy and optical clarity.
